skill.name: fiftyone-dataset-import
skill.description: Import datasets into FiftyOne by detecting media types, label formats, folder layouts, and multimodal grouping patterns. The skill supports images, videos, point clouds, 3D scenes, common annotation formats, and autonomous driving datasets through the FiftyOne MCP server.
skill.summary: Detect dataset structure and import multimodal data into FiftyOne.
skill.author: AdonaiVera

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but most high-severity matches are markdown false positives. The real risks are operational: the skill directs agents to inspect local datasets, discover or install dependencies, and load pickle annotation files. No evidence of malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe Pickle Deserialization in Import Examples
medium_findings.description: The skill shows workflows that load pickle files from dataset directories with pickle.load. Pickle can execute code during deserialization, so this is dangerous when datasets or annotations are untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct pickle.load calls on dataset files. The behavior is legitimate for some trusted formats, but it is a known code execution risk for untrusted data.

medium_findings.title: Agent-Directed Shell Commands and Package Installation
medium_findings.description: The static command findings are mostly markdown examples, not embedded Ruby execution. However, the skill intentionally directs agents to run shell commands, pip installs, git clone, and package checks, which can modify the environment or execute installer code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are shown in documentation blocks and the skill asks for permission before installation. The risk remains real because the intended workflow can execute external package installers.

medium_findings.title: Broad Local Filesystem Enumeration
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to recursively list files, sample annotation files, and inspect dataset directories. This is required for dataset detection, but it can expose local filenames or sensitive metadata if the user gives a broad path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is explicit and central to the skill. It is not malicious, but it has privacy impact when used on broad or sensitive directories.

low_findings.title: Network and Supply Chain Exposure for Format Discovery
low_findings.description: The skill recommends web searches, documentation lookup, GitHub package discovery, and GitHub-based pip installation. These network actions are useful for uncommon dataset formats but introduce dependency trust and provenance concerns.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill contains explicit network discovery and hardcoded documentation links. The cited URLs are relevant resources, but package discovery can lead to risky dependencies.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Many reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography hits are markdown inline code, tables, or ordinary words, not executable code. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration instruction was found in the reviewed skill text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are markdown prose, tables, or plugin names. They do not contain cryptographic operations, Ruby execution, or hidden control instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unsafe Pickle Deserialization in Import Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows workflows that load pickle files from dataset directories with pickle.load. Pickle can execute code during deserialization, so this is dangerous when datasets or annotations are untrusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file contains direct pickle.load calls on dataset files. The behavior is legitimate for some trusted formats, but it is a known code execution risk for untrusted data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent-Directed Shell Commands and Package Installation
dangerous_patterns.description: The static command findings are mostly markdown examples, not embedded Ruby execution. However, the skill intentionally directs agents to run shell commands, pip installs, git clone, and package checks, which can modify the environment or execute installer code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are shown in documentation blocks and the skill asks for permission before installation. The risk remains real because the intended workflow can execute external package installers.

content.user_title: Import FiftyOne Datasets From Many Formats
content.value_statement: Computer vision datasets often arrive with mixed media, labels, and folder layouts. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code inspect the structure, choose an import path, and create a FiftyOne dataset.
actual_capabilities: Scans dataset folders to identify media files, labels, and directory patterns.
Maps common formats such as COCO, YOLO, VOC, CVAT, KITTI, and OpenLABEL to FiftyOne import options.
Detects grouped multimodal layouts with multiple cameras, LiDAR, point clouds, or 3D scenes.
Guides package checks and optional dependency installation for specialized dataset formats.
Creates validation steps that compare imported sample counts with source files.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for existing datasets, missing labels, and grouped views.
limitations: Requires a running FiftyOne MCP server and relevant FiftyOne plugins.
Package installation and web lookup may need explicit user approval and network access.
Pickle-based dataset examples should only be used with trusted files.
Uncommon formats may require custom import code or external devkit documentation.

use_cases.title: Import annotated image datasets
use_cases.description: Load image datasets with common annotation formats into FiftyOne after detecting labels and media layout.

use_cases.title: Build grouped autonomous driving datasets
use_cases.description: Create grouped datasets from camera, LiDAR, and annotation files for scene-based review.

use_cases.title: Audit unknown dataset folders
use_cases.description: Inventory file types, labels, and missing dependencies before deciding how to import the dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Scan a dataset folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan this dataset folder and summarize the media types, label files, likely format, and any risks before import: /path/to/data

prompt_templates.title: Import a known format
prompt_templates.prompt: Import this dataset into FiftyOne if it looks like COCO, YOLO, VOC, CVAT, KITTI, or OpenLABEL. Confirm the plan with me before creating the dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Create a grouped dataset
prompt_templates.scenario: Multimodal scenes with camera and LiDAR data
prompt_templates.prompt: Detect whether this folder should become a grouped FiftyOne dataset. Identify slices, labels, default slice, and required packages before import.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a specialized format
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this autonomous driving dataset, identify the devkit required, check installed packages, and propose a safe import workflow without installing anything yet.

output_examples.input: User asks to scan an unlabeled folder before import.
output_examples.output: A concise scan summary listing media counts, detected label formats, grouping pattern, package needs, and a confirmation question.

output_examples.input: User asks to import a scene-based camera and LiDAR dataset.
output_examples.output: A proposed grouped dataset configuration with slices, default view, label fields, validation checks, and dependency warnings.

output_examples.input: User reports that the imported dataset has missing labels.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting plan that checks label paths, format detection, annotation counts, and FiftyOne field mappings.

best_practices: Use the narrowest dataset path possible to avoid scanning unrelated local files.
Review package sources before approving pip or GitHub installation commands.
Use pickle-based imports only for trusted datasets from known sources.
anti_patterns: Do not import a dataset before reviewing the scan summary and proposed configuration.
Do not install unknown devkits without checking the source and version.
Do not flatten multimodal scenes when the files represent synchronized group members.

faq.question: Does this skill require FiftyOne?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects the FiftyOne MCP server and related import utilities to be available.

faq.question: Can it import point clouds and 3D scenes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for point clouds, 3D scenes, and grouped multimodal datasets.

faq.question: Does it install packages automatically?
faq.answer: It instructs the agent to ask for permission before installing required packages.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted datasets?
faq.answer: Use caution. Some examples load pickle files, which are unsafe when the dataset source is not trusted.

faq.question: Which annotation formats are covered?
faq.answer: It covers common formats including COCO, YOLO, VOC, CVAT, KITTI, OpenLABEL, CSV, DICOM, and GeoJSON.

faq.question: Can it detect grouped data automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks scene folders, filename prefixes, and mixed media types to propose grouping.
